'''Norne''' was a [[Planet/Legends|world]] situated in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]. It featured several [[City/Legends|cities]] that were home to homeless [[Youngling/Legends|children]]. Norne was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Mygo Skinto]], who was orphaned and then, upon reaching the age of legal independence, forced to live on the streets due to her guardians having spent her inheritance. Skinto began to organize Norne's street children and eventually established a [[Mygo's Warren|permanent home]] for them. By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], she had also began traveling off-world in order to help the homeless children of other [[Star system/Legends|star systems]], as well.

==Description==
Norne was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|world]]<ref name="CRO" /> located in the [[Norne system/Legends|Norne system]] of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].<ref name="TEA" />

==History==
[[File:MygoSkinto.png|thumb|right|300px|Norne was the homeworld of Mygo Skinto (center).]]
Norne was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Mygo Skinto]], who was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]]<ref name="CRO" /> around [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]].<ref name="Date">''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'', which is set shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], gives [[Mygo Skinto]]'s age as twenty-two, therefore she must have been [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] around [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> The [[Yacht/Legends|yacht]] of Skinto's affluent [[Parent/Legends|parents]] disappeared when she was very young, leaving the ultimately orphaned [[Youngling/Legends|child]] to be [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttled]] between various relatives who were only really interested in spending her wealth instead of taking care of her.<ref name="CRO" />

When the well-educated Skinto reached the Norne age of independence, she was left destitute by her guardians having squandered her inheritance and was forced to live on the streets of her homeworld's [[City/Legends|cities]]. She wandered the streets, [[Thief/Legends|stealing]] [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]] from street [[Merchant/Legends|vendors]] and [[Sleep/Legends|sleeping]] in alleys. Meanwhile, the adult population of Norne's urban centers largely overlooked Skinto on the account of her traumatic childhood experiences having resulted in the cessation of her body's physical development at an early age, giving her the appearance of a child.<ref name="CRO" />

In contrast, the homeless children of the world's cities idolized Skinto and began to follow her. The woman in turn organized them, setting up routines for begging activities and negotiating agreements with the owners of stores. At some point, Skinto also established a [[Mygo's Warren|permanent home]] for the children. In addition, she eventually realized that children in other [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] were living in similarly challenging conditions, and she vowed to help them as well, beginning to travel off-world and, in the process of her efforts, becoming affiliated with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. By the [[Time/Legends|time]] shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Alliance [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] mentioned Norne in an entry dedicated to Skinto of a [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] detailing various Rebel operatives.<ref name="CRO" />

==Inhabitants==
Norne had a single age, no more than twenty-two [[Standard year/Legends|years]] old, at which an individual was considered legally independent.<ref name="CRO" />

==Locations==
Norne featured several cities with alleys, shops, and street vendors. Mygo Skinto also established a permanent home for street children under her care in the form of a warren-like habitat situated in ancient maintenance tunnels of a city.<ref name="CRO" />

==Behind the scenes==
Norne was created by [[Chuck Truett]] and was introduced in ''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'', a [[1994]] [[West End Games]] sourcebook published for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="CRO" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Norne system, and therefore the world itself, in grid square K-10.<ref name="TEA" />
